Market Intelligence Brief
Generated 10:39 ET
Defense stocks surge on call flow while institutions dump retail puts
KEY TAKEAWAYS
  • 1. Defense contractors see explosive call buying (+1008% RTX, +675% BA) ahead of earnings and geopolitical tensions
  • 2. The index put/single-name call split (1.84 vs 0.69) signals institutional dispersion hedging into GDP data
  • 3. Mega retail put dumps (NN +11801%, MAS +10527%) suggest earnings positioning unwinds
NARRATIVE — dispersion call preference

Defense contractors dominate unusual call flow with RTX spiking +1008% and BA +675%, positioning ahead of quarterly earnings amid elevated geopolitical risks. The aerospace theme extends beyond defense with GE (+560%) and GEV (+782%) joining the call-heavy cohort, suggesting sector rotation into industrial recovery plays. This concentrated sector betting contrasts sharply with broad retail sector put liquidation. Institutional flow reveals a classic dispersion setup through the extreme P/C divergence — index puts heavy at 1.84 while single-names show call preference at 0.69. With positive dealer gamma at $+4.4B dampening directional moves, sophisticated players are positioning for stock-specific outperformance rather than broad market directionality. The 0.03 implied correlation reading confirms this stock-picking environment, creating optimal conditions for dispersion trades. Massive retail put liquidation dominates the extremes with NN (+11801% puts), MAS (+10527% puts), and WELL (+10029% puts) seeing capitulation-level unwinds. These aren't bearish bets but rather position closures, likely driven by approaching GDP data in 2 days that could shift the retail consumption narrative. The IWM May 261P/264P block totaling 34,000 contracts suggests small-cap hedging into the economic data cascade.

Layer 1: Sentiment +51Layer 2: GEX +$4.4BLayer 3: P/C split extreme
RTXBULLISH
Defense earnings call surge
RTX leads defense contractor call buying with +1008% unusual volume ahead of quarterly earnings. Geopolitical tensions and defense spending tailwinds drive positioning into results. P/C ratio collapses to 0.25 as institutions load calls.

Retail put capitulation
NN sees extreme put liquidation at +11801% with two-way flow suggesting position unwinds rather than fresh bearish bets. Retail sector positioning adjusts ahead of GDP data that could shift consumption outlook. Calls also elevated at +815%.
L3: puts +11801%L3: calls +815%L9: GDP in 2 days

Small-cap GDP hedge
Massive IWM put block totaling 34,000 contracts across May 261P/264P strikes represents institutional hedging into GDP data cascade. Small-caps most sensitive to domestic economic shifts with data releases clustered over next week.
